claim: AI cannot reliably prioritize long-term business goals or assess trade-offs; humans must define vision and validate results
actionable: Design systems where AI suggests solutions but humans evaluate impact on business goals; don't delegate strategic decisions
source_title: Learning Software Engineering During AI Era

claim: 55% of developers use Copilot but only 30% accept outputs without changes; most don't trust AI-generated code quality
actionable: Plan for human review time in AI-assisted development workflows; don't assume generated code is production-ready
source_title: Learning Software Engineering During AI Era

claim: Software engineers who understand AI's internals (models, data pipelines, limitations) gain competitive advantage over those who only use AI
actionable: Build deep knowledge of LLM fundamentals, fine-tuning, and limitations to guide AI tool choices and implementations
source_title: Learning Software Engineering During AI Era

claim: Future engineers must be system architects, full-stack developers, and ethical technologists, not just fast coders
actionable: Develop breadth across system design, multiple disciplines, communication, and ethical reasoning alongside technical coding skills
source_title: Learning Software Engineering During AI Era

claim: AI raises the floor of what's achievable; software engineers must raise the ceiling by leading AI development
actionable: Focus on learning LLM fine-tuning, RAG, and prompt engineering to stay ahead as AI commoditizes basic coding tasks
source_title: Learning Software Engineering During AI Era
